name: Rocky Road Bars/Crisco
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C). Grease a 13 x 9 x 2-inch (3.5 L) pan with shortening. Set aside.
2. For the base, melt 2/3 cup (150 mL) Golden Crisco Shortening and 4 squares of Unsweetened chocolate in a large saucepan on low heat. Remove from heat. Stir in 2 cups (500 mL) granulated sugar. Add eggs one at a time, stirring quickly into the hot mixture. Stir in 1 teaspoon (5 mL) vanilla extract.
3. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually stir into the chocolate mixture. Spread in the baking pan.
4. Bake for 30 minutes at 350°F (180°C).
5. For the topping, prepare it 5 minutes before the base is finished baking. Melt 1/4 cup (50 mL) Golden Crisco Shortening and 1 square of Unsweetened chocolate in a medium saucepan on very low heat. Add evaporated milk and 1/2 cup (125 mL) granulated sugar. Stir until well blended and sugar dissolves. Remove from heat.
6. Beat in 1 3/4 cups Icing sugar and 1/2 teaspoon (2 mL) vanilla extract.
7. Drop peanut butter by small spoonfuls on top of the hot chocolate mixture. Swirl through with the tip of a knife.
8. Sprinkle marshmallows over the hot baked base.
9. Pour the chocolate peanut butter topping over the marshmallows. Cover the pan immediately with foil. Cool. Cut into bars about 2 x 1-1/2 inches (5 x 4 cm). Makes: 36 bars. Note: If the topping becomes too firm to pour, reheat it on low.
Tip: Golden Crisco Shortening cookie recipes are most easily and quickly prepared with an electric mixer. However, all of our recipes may also be mixed by hand.
